title: 3n + 7 is injective
informal_statement: The function n |-> 3n + 7 on natural numbers is injective.
informal_proof_idea: `simp [Function.Injective]` unfolds the definition to the implication `3 * a + 7 = 3 * b + 7 -> a = b` and closes it directly via simp's default arithmetic-cancellation simp set.
formal_statement_pp: theorem linear_injective_concrete_instance : Function.Injective (fun n : ℕ => 3 * n + 7)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 9c3f7163, problem_version 111758fa-4f09-4b9a-8c75-981bd370d773). Originally attempted as the negative example queued in packets/negative/functions/QUEUE.md ('simp-unfold on Function.Injective leaves an open goal'): the intended natural-but-wrong attempt (`simp [Function.Injective]` with no follow-up algebra) actually kernel-verified on the first step -- simp's default simp set closes the resulting linear cancellation directly. Per this domain's own queue policy, a hypothesis that turns out provable is not a valid negative example -- authored as a positive packet instead. domain: algebra is intentional, matching every other packet filed under the `functions` folder, since `functions` is not a valid domain enum value in schema/packet.schema.json.
